title: Let's Explore Brighthelm - a few turns of Paupers' Ladder with the Moon Towers expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BsWIaYs09vc

Daniel (@dungeondive) plays a casual few turns of Pauper's Ladder with the Moon Towers expansion — not a how-to-play, just a stroll through Brighthelm. He's Darius Burr, a fisherman from Grey Haven whose fish-hook ability lets him bank a "fish" gem each time he enters a beach region to sell back in the city, paired with a pigeon companion (untrainable until you pay four gems), a fighting-tea item, three recipes, and a starting talent — he picks Beggar over Cook.

Running the solo mode (popper and bird each at 5 health, a 15-step time track he can replenish), he demonstrates the loop: fishing the beach, exploring decks, getting cursed with Butterfingers at a Willow's Hovel (then cleared free in a city with under two gems), training the pigeon into a money-gathering scout, and felling an Iron Eye and a White Pirate for gems, ingredients or trophies. He cashes the dead pirate's combing rod, banks crescents off a tough Moon Warden, and threads it all through the lovely Illustrated Field Guide — colouring in Darius and ticking achievements (Iron Eye, trained pigeon, combing rod) as he goes.

A warm showcase of how effortlessly the game's charm and small bookkeeping come together at the table.

----

Watching it in motion — does Pauper's Ladder's gentle, exploratory rhythm look like your kind of solo evening, or too light to hold you?
